description: The cryptography library is a Python package that provides cryptographic recipes and primitives to developers.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/cryptography && python3 cryptography.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/cryptography && python3 cryptography.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/cryptography && python3 cryptography.py --help`. Cryptographic recipes and primitives (Fernet, RSA, X.509, TLS, ...).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the crypto category. Fits when the engagement needs: Cryptographic recipes and primitives (Fernet, RSA, X.509, TLS, ...).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Cryptographic recipes and primitives (Fernet, RSA, X.509, TLS, ...).
